title: CJ McCollum's Late Fadeaway Lifts Hawks Over Knicks 109-108 To Take Game 3
summary: CJ McCollum converted a 15-foot fadeaway jumper with 12.5 seconds remaining Thursday night, giving the Atlanta Hawks a 109-108 victory over the New York Knicks in Game 3. Atlanta leads the first-round series 2-1 heading into Saturday's Game 4. McCollum, who tormented New York in Game 2 at Madison Square Garden, finished with 23 points. Jalen Johnson paced the Hawks with 24 points while Jonathan Kuminga contributed 21 off the bench. "They work, they share," said Hawks coach Quin Snyder. "That's the thing about this group that I've enjoyed so much." Atlanta led by as many as 18 points in the first half before New York mounted a late charge. Jalen Brunson's 3-point play gave the Knicks a 108-105 edge with 1:03 remaining. Jalen Johnson answered with a basket, cutting the deficit to one and setting up McCollum's decisive shot. New York had two opportunities to win at the end but came up empty. Josh Hart missed a 3-pointer, the Knicks failed to beat the shot clock on the offensive rebound, and Brunson lost the ball to Kuminga as time expired. OG Anunoby led New York with 29 points. Brunson added 26 and Karl-Anthony Towns contributed 21. "You couldn't ask for anything better than that," Knicks coach Mike Brown said of the late-game situation. "Less than a minute to go and a chance to go up by three." The Knicks are -2.5 point favorites in Game 4 at Atlanta on Saturday . The Hawks have jumped to the favorites to win the series with implied odds of 57 percent.

title: Devin Booker Fined $35,000 For Criticism Of Refs, NBA Rescinds Technical
summary: Phoenix Suns guard Devin Booker has been fined $35,000 for public criticism of the officiating. Following an investigation including multiple interviews and video review, the league found no basis to any claim of bias or misconduct by game officials. Booker made his comments to the press following the Suns' 120-107 loss to the Oklahoma City Thunder in Game 2 of their first-round playoff series on April 22 at Paycom Center. "In my 11 years, I haven't called a ref out by name, but James [Williams] was terrible tonight through and through," Booker said on Wednesday . "It's bad for the sport, bad for the integrity of the sport. People are going to start viewing this as a WWE if they're not held responsible." The NBA also determined that Booker's technical foul at 2:05 in the third quarter was improperly assessed and it has therefore been rescinded.

title: Jalen Williams Ruled Out Indefinitely With Grade 1 Hamstring Strain
summary: Oklahoma City Thunder forward Jalen Williams will miss at least the next three games of the team's first-round series against the Phoenix Suns after sustaining a Grade 1 left hamstring strain, the team announced Thursday. Williams will be evaluated on a weekly basis. The injury occurred with 6:26 remaining in the third quarter of Wednesday's 120-107 Game 2 victory. Williams grabbed his left leg after missing a fast-break layup attempt, subsequently took a deliberate foul to exit the game and went directly to the locker room. He did not address reporters before departing Paycom Center. Williams is ruled out for Saturday's Game 3, Monday's Game 4 and a potential Wednesday Game 5. The timing is a significant blow despite Oklahoma City's commanding series position. Williams had been outstanding to open the postseason after a regular season limited to 33 games due to injury. He posted 22 points, seven rebounds and six assists in Game 1 and followed with 19 points on 7-of-11 shooting in 23 minutes in Game 2. "He was playing an outstanding game," coach Mark Daigneault said. "He was on the gas. He had great force. He was hitting shots." Williams, a third-team All-NBA selection last season, played through a torn scapholunate ligament in his right wrist throughout last year's championship run before undergoing surgery. He also missed extended stretches this season with a right hamstring strain. Oklahoma City went 39-10 without Williams in the lineup this regular season.

title: Victor Wembanyama Traveling With Spurs To Portland, Status For Game 3 Uncertain
summary: Victor Wembanyama will accompany the San Antonio Spurs to Portland this weekend while continuing to work through the NBA's concussion protocol. Coach Mitch Johnson described the center as "progressing" but stopped short of clearing him for Game 3 Friday against the Portland Trail Blazers. Wembanyama sustained the concussion during Tuesday's Game 2 loss to Portland, exiting in the second quarter. The series is tied 1-1, with Game 3 on Friday and Game 4 on Sunday, both in Portland. The Spurs are -3.5 point favorites for Game 3 . The NBA's return-to-play protocol requires players to complete sequential steps, beginning with light exertion such as stationary cycling, jogging, agility work and non-contact drills, each followed by a neurological examination. Results are measured against a pre-season baseline evaluation before clearance is granted. The 7-foot-4 French center, who became the league's first unanimous Defensive Player of the Year and is among three MVP finalists, anchors everything the Spurs do. San Antonio finished with the NBA's second-best regular season record and went 12-6 in games without him. Wembanyama averaged 25 points, 11.5 rebounds, 3.1 assists and a league-leading 3.1 blocks per game this season.

title: Derrick White Wins 2025-26 NBA Sportsmanship Award
summary: Boston Celtics guard Derrick White has been selected as the 2025-26 NBA Sportsmanship Award recipient . White claims the Joe Dumars Trophy for the first time in his nine-year career. The honor marks consecutive seasons in which a Celtic has earned the award as Jrue Holiday received the trophy last season. While Holiday is a two-time winner, Mike Conley has won the award four times. Current NBA players cast votes to determine the winner from six divisional finalists. A panel of league executives selected those finalists from 30 team nominees, one per club. The Joe Dumars Trophy has been presented annually since the 1995-96 season. It honors the player who best exemplifies sportsmanship on the court and is named for Naismith Basketball Hall of Famer Joe Dumars, who won the inaugural award and spent his entire 14-year career with the Detroit Pistons. White brings a decorated resume to the recognition. A two-time NBA All-Defensive Team selection, he won an NBA championship with Boston in 2023-24 and captured a gold medal with the USA Men's National Team at the 2024 Paris Olympics.

title: Steve Kerr Decision Hasn't Been Made As Internal Optimism Wanes
summary: Optimism within the Golden State Warriors organization about Steve Kerr's return has dimmed in recent weeks, multiple team sources told The Athletic. Staffers have begun preparing for a potential coaching change, though sources stressed no decision is final until Kerr meets with owner Joe Lacob and general manager Mike Dunleavy. ESPN reported Monday that the Warriors would require Kerr to sign a multi-year extension if he returns. One team source neither confirmed nor denied that detail. Speculation around the league regarding possible successors has already surfaced. League and team sources said Kerr's desire to speak openly on social and political matters caused internal friction at points throughout the season. Veteran journalist Marc Spears said this week that Kerr has felt "stifled" at times, though whether that factor proves decisive remains uncertain. Stephen Curry, who will earn nearly $63 million next season and indicated openness to a contract extension, declined to publicly urge Kerr to stay. "I want Coach to be happy," Curry said. "I want him to be excited about the job. Whatever that means for him, everybody's plan is their own." Kerr has been transparent that he has a strong desire to continue coaching Curry. "I don't want to walk away from Steph," Kerr said following Friday's season-ending loss to the Phoenix Suns. "I'm definitely not going and coaching somewhere else next year in the NBA. I would never walk away from Steph, but all the stuff has to be aligned and right."

title: Karl-Anthony Towns Wants To Be 'A Knick For Life'
summary: Karl-Anthony Towns has expressed a strong desire to remain with the New York Knicks long-term, according to a source close to the big man. However, his postseason performance and fit within coach Mike Brown's system are drawing sharp scrutiny around the league. "He so badly wants things to work here," a source close to Towns told ESPN. "He wants to be a Knick for life." Despite that desire, rival executives warn Towns faces significant pressure. The Knicks, under a direct mandate from owner James Dolan, are expected to reach the franchise's first NBA Finals since 1999 . Towns carries two years remaining on his contract, worth $57.7 million in 2026-27 with a $61 million player option for 2027-28. That salary makes him New York's largest potential trade asset should the organization pursue Milwaukee Bucks forward Giannis Antetokounmpo. Towns had interest in extending before the start of the current season, but didn't expect the Knicks to offer one. League sources indicate the Bucks and Knicks held exclusive negotiations centered on Antetokounmpo last summer. With Milwaukee's turbulent season, further exploration of the two-time MVP's future is widely anticipated. The way Towns is utilized remains a persistent debate. His 13.8 shot attempts this regular season marked a career low, while scoring dropped to 20.1 points per game, his lowest since his rookie year. Several Eastern Conference assistants have questioned how Brown's staff has deployed him. The stakes for Towns personally are considerable. "If they come up short, if they don't make the Finals -- aside from Bridges, because he was traded for all the draft picks -- Towns will take the most heat because of his resume," a high-ranking rival team executive said. "Because Brunson won't take the heat and they'll say Towns didn't deliver. Especially with Giannis sitting out there."

title: Devin Booker, Dillon Brooks Blast Officiating After Suns' Game 2 Loss To Thunder
summary: Devin Booker publicly criticized a referee by name following Phoenix's 120-107 Game 2 defeat to Oklahoma City on Wednesday. The loss gives the defending champion Thunder a commanding 2-0 first-round series lead. "In my 11 years, I haven't called a ref out by name, but James [Williams] was terrible tonight through and through," Booker said. "It's bad for the sport, bad for the integrity of the sport. People are going to start viewing this as a WWE if they're not held responsible." Booker took particular issue with a technical foul assessed against him at 2:05 of the third quarter, which he said came without explanation. "This is my first time in 11 years, but it's needed," Booker said. "Whatever I get fined for it, everybody can pull the clips and see where the frustration is from." Forward Dillon Brooks echoed the complaints, directing his criticism at foul calls benefiting reigning MVP Shai Gilgeous-Alexander. Brooks scored 30 points before fouling out late. Gilgeous-Alexander led all scorers with 37 points, connecting on all nine free throw attempts. "[Gilgeous-Alexander is] a little frail, and that's what the refs are going to call," Brooks said. "This is the playoffs. It's a man's game." Gilgeous-Alexander deflected the criticism gracefully while praising Brooks as a competitor. "I can't control what Dillon or anybody else on the other side is going to complain about," Gilgeous-Alexander said. "All I can do is try to go out there and win basketball games for my team." Game 3 shifts to Phoenix with the Suns 9.5 point underdogs against the defending champion Thunder .

title: Pistons End 11-Game Home Playoff Losing Streak With Dominant Game 2 Win Over Magic
summary: A fierce halftime speech from J.B. Bickerstaff sparked the Detroit Pistons as they had a 30-3 third-quarter run to defeat the Orlando Magic 98-83, leveling the first-round series at 1-1 on Wednesday night. The victory ended Detroit's NBA-record 11-game home playoff losing streak. The Pistons' last home postseason win had come on May 26, 2008, against Boston in the Eastern Conference finals. "He really got on us in the locker room," said Tobias Harris. "[His message was] there is no more of 'my bads.' It's like they're out there hustling, getting offensive boards on us. And there's too many of them for us [to allow] as a group. We know that's not our standard. So he was on us. We were able to find that little spark." Detroit's defense proved decisive. The Pistons recorded 11 total blocks and held Orlando to just 18% shooting on contested attempts, the lowest such figure in postseason play since ESPN began tracking the stat in 2013-14. Cade Cunningham led Detroit, finishing with 27 points, 11 assists and six rebounds after a 39-point showing in Game 1. "It's Piston's basketball, and that's what it looks like," Bickerstaff said. "We have one off night [in Game 1 that happened] to come at a bad time. But I know what our guys are, and we trusted that they were going to come back tonight, be the best version of themselves at some point." Orlando's Paolo Banchero tallied 18 points, eight assists and six rebounds despite the loss. "We're not backing down," Magic coach Jamahl Mosley said. "When we go to Orlando, we got to just make sure we exceed it." Game 3 tips off Saturday in Orlando with the Pistons 3.5 point favorites over the Magic .

title: Jalen Williams Exits Game 2 Win Over Suns With Left Hamstring Injury
summary: Oklahoma City Thunder wing Jalen Williams left Wednesday night's 120-107 Game 2 victory over the Phoenix Suns with an apparent left hamstring injury in the third quarter and did not return, casting uncertainty over the remainder of the first-round series. "We think he aggravated his left hamstring," Mark Daigneault said. "We'll take a look at it in the next couple of days and update you guys appropriately." Williams grabbed his left leg after missing a contested layup on a fast break with 6:26 remaining in the third quarter, appeared to mouth the words "Left hammy," and intentionally drew a foul shortly after to exit the game. He went directly to the locker room and did not speak with media before leaving Paycom Center. Williams had been exceptional through two postseason games after injuries restricted him to just 33 regular-season appearances. He posted 22 points, seven rebounds, and six assists in Game 1, then added 19 points on 7-of-11 shooting and four assists in 23 minutes before exiting Wednesday. "Any assumption about time missed is just hypothetical at this point, so I'm not going to comment on that," Daigneault said. "He was playing an outstanding game." Williams, a third-team All-NBA selection last season, played through a torn scapholunate ligament in his right wrist during Oklahoma City's championship run before undergoing surgery. He missed the first 19 regular-season games recovering from that procedure and twice sat out extended stretches this season with a right hamstring strain. Oklahoma City leads the series 2-0. Game 3 is Saturday in Phoenix with the Thunder favored by 9.5 points over the Suns .

title: Adam Silver Supports Grizzlies Playing Select Games In Nashville While Backing Memphis Long-Term
summary: NBA Commissioner Adam Silver expressed confidence in the Memphis Grizzlies' long-term viability Wednesday while acknowledging he would welcome a greater franchise presence in Nashville, responding directly to recent remarks by LeBron James suggesting the team relocate. Speaking on Barstool's "Pardon My Take" podcast, Silver confirmed that Grizzlies governor Robert Pera has expressed no interest in leaving Memphis. Silver did, however, float the idea of the club playing occasional games in the state capital. "Nashville is a city on the rise," Silver said. "If it were up to me, I'd love for them to play a few games a year in Nashville and sort of be Tennessee's team to the extent they can." James sparked the discussion earlier in April during a "Bob Does Sports" appearance, stating that NBA players widely dislike visiting Memphis and that Nashville, home to the NHL's Predators and NFL's Titans, would be a more suitable destination. "Go over to Nashville," James said. "They got everything." Silver disputed James' characterization of player sentiment toward Memphis. "Players I talk to all the time like playing in Memphis," Silver said. "Memphis has been a great market, historically for the NBA, amazing history in that city, amazing culture." There is regional precedent for split home schedules. The San Antonio Spurs have played two regular-season games at Austin's Moody Center in each of the past four seasons as part of their I-35 Series. However, Memphis and Nashville are separated by 213 miles, compared to just 77 miles between San Antonio and Austin. The Grizzlies' FedExForum lease expires after the 2028-29 season. The facility opened in 2004, three years after the franchise relocated from Vancouver. "From my standpoint, there's no reason why the Memphis Grizzlies can't be successful," Silver said.

title: Raptors Parent Company Plans $18.25 Billion Sports Entity After MLSE Buyout
summary: Rogers Communications, majority owner of the Toronto Raptors through its 75% stake in Maple Leaf Sports & Entertainment, announced plans Wednesday to complete its full acquisition of MLSE and consolidate its sports holdings into a single entity valued at more than $18.25 billion. CEO Tony Staffieri confirmed on the company's first-quarter earnings call that Rogers expects to purchase the remaining 25% MLSE minority interest, held by Larry Tanenbaum's Kilmer Sports Ventures, in the second half of 2025. "Following the close, we plan to combine our assets into one of the most significant sports ownership, media and entertainment entities globally," Staffieri said. Rogers acquired BCE's 37.5% MLSE stake in 2025, bringing its ownership to 75%. MLSE controls the NBA's Raptors, NHL's Maple Leafs, and MLS's Toronto FC. Rogers separately owns MLB's Blue Jays. The company intends to bring in minority investors for the consolidated sports group, using proceeds to reduce debt. CFO Glenn Brandt said the recapitalization process is expected to conclude in late 2026 or early 2027. Current Sportico valuations place the Raptors at $4.66 billion, the Maple Leafs at $4.25 billion, the Blue Jays at $2.9 billion, and Toronto FC at $730 million, a combined $12.5 billion. The broader entity would also incorporate Rogers' Sportsnet media assets. Rogers stock surged nearly 14% Wednesday, closing at $37.48, following guidance of reduced capital spending and stronger free cash flow. First-quarter revenue and earnings largely met analyst expectations.

title: Aspiration Founder Cooperated With NBA's Kawhi Leonard Salary Cap Investigation
summary: Joseph Sanberg, the Aspiration co-founder facing federal sentencing April 27, provided substantial cooperation to the NBA's ongoing salary cap circumvention investigation involving Kawhi Leonard and the Los Angeles Clippers, the league's lead investigator confirmed in a court letter. David Anders of Wachtell Lipton stated in an April 17 filing that Sanberg sat for two in-person interviews, supplied relevant documents, and delivered information consistent with the league's broader review of evidence. "Mr. Sanberg's cooperation substantially assisted our investigation, including our ability to develop a more complete understanding of key events," Anders wrote. The NBA launched its probe in September 2025 following reports that Leonard's $28 million Aspiration sponsorship was structured to circumvent the salary cap. Clippers owner Steve Ballmer had invested $50 million in the company in 2021, with the club separately announcing a $300 million, 23-year sponsorship agreement. Ballmer made an additional $10 million investment in March 2023. Sanberg previously pleaded guilty to two counts of wire fraud, with prosecutors alleging he defrauded investors of $248 million. Each count carries a maximum 20-year sentence. His attorneys are seeking leniency, describing him as motivated by purpose rather than personal gain. In a separate court letter, Ballmer's attorney stated the Clippers owner lost his entire $60 million Aspiration investment after the company declared bankruptcy in 2025, and has suffered extensive reputational damage. The Clippers lost nearly all $300 million in sponsorship payments and more than $20 million held in escrow. Ballmer has additionally been named in a civil lawsuit by 11 former Aspiration investors. He has denied all allegations and sought dismissal of the case.

title: Keldon Johnson Wins 2025-26 NBA Sixth Man Of The Year Award
summary: San Antonio Spurs guard-forward Keldon Johnson has won the 2025-26 NBA Sixth Man of the Year Award , earning the John Havlicek Trophy after a standout season off the bench. Johnson averaged 13.2 points and 5.4 rebounds in 23.3 minutes per game, becoming the only player in the league to appear in all 82 games as a reserve. He shot 51.9% from the field, his most efficient mark since his rookie campaign in 2019-20. His 1,081 bench points set a San Antonio franchise record and ranked second league-wide. The Spurs finished with the NBA's second-best record at 62-20. A panel of 100 global media voters determined the award winner. Miami Heat forward Jamie Jaquez Jr. finished second with 34 first-place votes and 51 second-place votes. Tim Hardaway Jr. placed third, followed by Naz Reid and Ajay Mitchell. Johnson becomes the second Spur to claim the honor, joining Manu Ginobili, who won the award in 2007-08. To qualify for consideration, a player must have come off the bench in more games than he started during the regular season.

summary: Steve Kerr has been meeting with media agents as his Golden State Warriors contract approaches expiration, with broadcasting, front office work, and a return to coaching all remaining possibilities, sources told Front Office Sports. Kerr has not yet settled on a next step. A spokesperson for his coaching agency, Priority Sports, declined to comment. All three current NBA broadcast partners, ESPN, NBC, and Amazon, would pursue conversations with Kerr should he choose to re-enter the media space, per the report. Kerr brings rare credentials to any potential broadcasting role. He won nine NBA championships total, five as a player, including three with Michael Jordan's Chicago Bulls and two with the San Antonio Spurs, and four more as Golden State's head coach. Kerr has also already demonstrated elite ability behind the microphone. Kerr served as TNT's lead color analyst in two separate stints alongside play-by-play voice Marv Albert, from 2003-07 and again from 2010-14, with a term as Phoenix Suns general manager between the two runs. Those broadcasts showcased sharp tactical analysis, polished timing, and a sense of humor, qualities that would make him a sought-after addition to any NBA rights holder's talent roster. Kerr would reenter broadcasting with an even higher profile and direct familiarity with the modern game.

title: Darius Acuff Jr. Declares For 2026 NBA Draft
summary: Arkansas guard Darius Acuff Jr. announced Wednesday on ESPN's "NBA Today" that he is entering the 2026 NBA Draft. The 6-foot-3 point guard is currently ranked the sixth-best prospect available, per ESPN's Jeremy Woo. "With that being said, I'm going to be declaring for the 2026 NBA draft," Acuff said. Acuff had a record-setting freshman campaign with the Razorbacks, earning SEC Player of the Year honors and a spot on the Associated Press All-America first team. He averaged 23.5 points, 6.4 rebounds, and 3.1 assists while shooting 44% from three-point range, leading Arkansas to the Sweet 16. The announcement continues a notable pattern under coach John Calipari. Derrick Rose, John Wall, De'Aaron Fox, and Shai Gilgeous-Alexander are among the lottery picks who developed under Calipari at Memphis and Kentucky. Acuff cited that track record as a factor in his decision to sign with Arkansas. NBA Hall of Famer Allen Iverson also played a formative role in Acuff's development and style of play. The two have built a relationship in recent months. "We've got a lot of similarities. He's one of the reasons I got cornrows, so he inspired me a lot," Acuff said. "He always told me to just play every game like it's your last." Acuff said watching Tuesday's Los Angeles Lakers-Houston Rockets playoff game reinforced his ambitions at the next level. "I see myself there, for sure. That's one of my goals," he said.

title: Marcus Smart Dominates Kevin Durant, Lakers Take 2-0 Series Lead Over Rockets
summary: Marcus Smart delivered a standout two-way performance Tuesday, holding Kevin Durant in check while scoring 25 points to lead the Los Angeles Lakers to a 101-94 Game 2 victory over the Houston Rockets. Durant, returning from a right knee injury that sidelined him for Game 1, finished with 23 points. However, he shot just 1-for-3 and committed three of his playoff career-worst nine turnovers across 18 possessions guarded by Smart, per ESPN Research. Smart finished with 8-for-13 shooting, five 3-pointers, seven assists, and five steals. His fifth three-pointer, with 2:23 remaining, extended a five-point advantage to eight and effectively sealed the outcome. "Smart, he just had a killer game today," said Lakers coach JJ Redick.
